reasons: Lolita protects Wanwan from projectile and ranged pressure while Wanwan looks for weakness angles.
This protection helps Wanwan survive the vulnerable setup phase before Crossbow of Tang is available.

proof.summary: Wanwan needs time and spacing to hit all weakness points, while Lolita can protect her from ranged pressure during that setup window.
worksBestWhen: Lolita shields toward the enemy marksman, poke mage, or projectile-heavy threat.
Wanwan stays behind Lolita while preparing weakness hits.
failureCases: Enemies dive from side angles that bypass Lolita's shield.
Wanwan overextends before Lolita can protect her.
The enemy uses non-projectile control to reach Wanwan.

reasons: Minotaur gives Wanwan a frontline, healing, and CC layer so she can move and attack with less disruption.
His teamfight presence helps Wanwan enter fights after enemies are already controlled.

proof.summary: Minotaur creates space and sustain in front of Wanwan, giving her more time to hit weakness points and scale into the fight.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur protects Wanwan from divers instead of engaging too far away.
Wanwan uses Minotaur's CC window to reposition and attack safely.
failureCases: Minotaur engages too deep and leaves Wanwan exposed.
Wanwan is bursted before she can use the frontline space.
The enemy forces Wanwan away before she opens enough weaknesses.

reasons: Angela can attach to Wanwan when Wanwan is being pressured, giving shield, sustain, and movement support.
This support helps Wanwan survive before her ultimate is unlocked.

proof.summary: Wanwan needs enough time to hit weakness points, and Angela can protect her during the vulnerable pre-ultimate window.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches when Wanwan is being dived or focused.
Wanwan still has room to kite after Angela attaches.
failureCases: Wanwan is chain-controlled before Angela can support her.
Angela attaches after Wanwan has already lost her safe position.
The enemy saves burst until Angela's protection ends.

reasons: Floryn can globally heal Wanwan while Wanwan kites, repositions, or fights away from the support.
This sustain helps Wanwan survive the time needed to unlock Crossbow of Tang.

proof.summary: Wanwan needs time to hit all weaknesses before using Crossbow of Tang, and Floryn can support that window with global healing.
worksBestWhen: Floryn saves Bloom for the moment Wanwan is being focused.
Wanwan still has enough space to kite after receiving the heal.
failureCases: Wanwan is bursted before the heal changes the fight.
Wanwan is suppressed or chain-controlled after her escape skill is unavailable.
Floryn heals too early before Wanwan is actually pressured.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag one target, giving Wanwan a more stable target to open weakness points.
Wanwan can use the target-lock window to attack safely and prepare Crossbow of Tang.

proof.summary: Kaja locks one enemy with suppression, while Wanwan uses the control window to safely attack and open weakness points.
worksBestWhen: Kaja suppresses a target that Wanwan can immediately reach.
Wanwan is already positioned to hit multiple weakness angles.
Kaja drags the target toward Wanwan's attack path.
failureCases: The suppression window ends before Wanwan opens enough weaknesses.
Kaja catches a tank or low-value target.
Kaja drags the target into an angle that makes Wanwan's weakness hits harder.
